sections.label: Section 466
sections.original: Διόνυσος ἡμᾶς εἰσέβησʼ , ὁ τοῦ Διός .
sections.translation: Dionysus , the child of Zeus , sent me .

sections.label: Section 467
sections.original: Ζεὺς δʼ ἔστʼ ἐκεῖ τις , ὃς νέους τίκτει θεούς ;
sections.translation: Is there a Zeus who breeds new gods there ?

sections.label: Section 468
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ ὁ Σεμέλην ἐνθάδε ζεύξας γάμοις .
sections.translation: No , but the one who married Semele here .

sections.label: Section 469
sections.original: πότερα δὲ νύκτωρ σʼ ἢ κατʼ ὄμμʼ ἠνάγκασεν ;
sections.translation: Did he compel you at night , or in your sight ?

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: ὁρῶν ὁρῶντα , καὶ δίδωσιν ὄργια .
sections.translation: Seeing me just as I saw him , he gave me sacred rites .

sections.label: Section 471
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ὄργιʼ ἐστὶ τίνʼ ἰδέαν ἔχοντά σοι ;
sections.translation: What appearance do your rites have ?

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: ἄρρητʼ ἀβακχεύτοισιν εἰδέναι βροτῶν .
sections.translation: They can not be told to mortals uninitiated in Bacchic revelry .

sections.label: Section 473
sections.original: ἔχει δʼ ὄνησιν τοῖσι θύουσιν τίνα ;
sections.translation: And do they have any profit to those who sacrifice ?

sections.label: Section 474
sections.original: οὐ θέμις ἀκοῦσαί σʼ , ἔστι δʼ ἄξιʼ εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: It is not lawful for you to hear , but they are worth knowing .

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: εὖ τοῦτʼ ἐκιβδήλευσας , ἵνʼ ἀκοῦσαι θέλω .
sections.translation: You have counterfeited this well , so that I desire to hear .

sections.label: Section 476
sections.original: ἀσέβειαν ἀσκοῦντʼ ὄργιʼ ἐχθαίρει θεοῦ .
sections.translation: The rites are hostile to whoever practices impiety .

sections.label: Section 477
sections.original: τὸν θεὸν ὁρᾶν γὰρ φῂς σαφῶς , ποῖός τις ἦν ;
sections.translation: Are you saying that you saw clearly what the god was like ?

sections.label: Section 478
sections.original: ὁποῖος ἤθελʼ · οὐκ ἐγὼ ʼτασσον τόδε .
sections.translation: He was as he chose ; I did not order this .

sections.label: Section 479
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὖ παρωχέτευσας εὖ κοὐδὲν λέγων .
sections.translation: Again you diverted my question well , speaking mere nonsense .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: δόξει τις ἀμαθεῖ σοφὰ λέγων οὐκ εὖ φρονεῖν .
sections.translation: One will seem to be foolish if he speaks wisely to an ignorant man .

sections.label: Section 481
sections.original: ἦλθες δὲ πρῶτα δεῦρʼ ἄγων τὸν δαίμονα ;
sections.translation: Did you come here first , bringing the god ?

sections.label: Section 482
sections.original: πᾶς ἀναχορεύει βαρβάρων τάδʼ ὄργια .
sections.translation: All the barbarians celebrate these rites .

sections.label: Section 483
sections.original: φρονοῦσι γὰρ κάκιον Ἑλλήνων πολύ .
sections.translation: Yes , for they are far more foolish than Hellenes .

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: τάδʼ εὖ γε μᾶλλον · οἱ νόμοι δὲ διάφοροι .
sections.translation: In this at any rate they are wiser ; but their laws are different .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ἱερὰ νύκτωρ ἢ μεθʼ ἡμέραν τελεῖς ;
sections.translation: Do you perform the rites by night or by day ?

sections.label: Section 486
sections.original: νύκτωρ τὰ πολλά · σεμνότητʼ ἔχει σκότος .
sections.translation: Mostly by night ; darkness conveys awe .

sections.label: Section 487
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ἐς γυναῖκας δόλιόν ἐστι καὶ σαθρόν .
sections.translation: This is treacherous towards women , and unsound .

sections.label: Section 488
sections.original: κἀν ἡμέρᾳ τό γʼ αἰσχρὸν ἐξεύροι τις ἄν .
sections.translation: Even during the day someone may devise what is shameful .

sections.label: Section 489
sections.original: δίκην σε δοῦναι δεῖ σοφισμάτων κακῶν .
sections.translation: You must pay the penalty for your evil contrivances .

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: σὲ δʼ ἀμαθίας γε κἀσεβοῦντʼ ἐς τὸν θεόν .
sections.translation: And you for your ignorance and impiety toward the god .

sections.label: Section 491
sections.original: ὡς θρασὺς ὁ βάκχος κοὐκ ἀγύμναστος λόγων .
sections.translation: How bold the Bacchant is , and not unpracticed in speaking !

sections.label: Section 492
sections.original: εἴφʼ ὅ τι παθεῖν δεῖ · τί με τὸ δεινὸν ἐργάσῃ ;
sections.translation: Tell me what I must suffer ; what harm will you do to me ?

sections.label: Section 493
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν ἁβρὸν βόστρυχον τεμῶ σέθεν .
sections.translation: First I will cut off your delicate hair .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: ἱερὸς ὁ πλόκαμος · τῷ θεῷ δʼ αὐτὸν τρέφω .
sections.translation: My hair is sacred . I am growing it for the god .

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: ἔπειτα θύρσον τόνδε παράδος ἐκ χεροῖν .
sections.translation: Next give me this thyrsos from your hands .

sections.label: Section 496
sections.original: αὐτός μʼ ἀφαιροῦ · τόνδε Διονύσου φορῶ .
sections.translation: Take it from me yourself . I bear it as the ensign of Dionysus .

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original: εἱρκταῖσί τʼ ἔνδον σῶμα σὸν φυλάξομεν .
sections.translation: We will guard your body within , in prison .

sections.label: Section 498
sections.original: λύσει μʼ ὁ δαίμων αὐτός , ὅταν ἐγὼ θέλω .
sections.translation: The god himself will release me , whenever I want .

sections.label: Section 499
sections.original: ὅταν γε καλέσῃς αὐτὸν ἐν βάκχαις σταθείς .
sections.translation: Yes , when you call him , standing among the Bacchae .

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἃ πάσχω πλησίον παρὼν ὁρᾷ .
sections.translation: Even now he see my sufferings from close by .

sections.label: Section 501
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼστιν ; οὐ γὰρ φανερὸς ὄμμασίν γʼ ἐμοῖς .
sections.translation: Where is he ? He is not visible to my eyes .

sections.label: Section 502
sections.original: παρʼ ἐμοί · σὺ δʼ ἀσεβὴς αὐτὸς ὢν οὐκ εἰσορᾷς .
sections.translation: Near me ; but you , being impious , do not see him .

sections.label: Section 503
sections.original: λάζυσθε · καταφρονεῖ με καὶ Θήβας ὅδε .
sections.translation: To attendants Seize him ; he insults me and Thebes !

sections.label: Section 504
sections.original: αὐδῶ με μὴ δεῖν σωφρονῶν οὐ σώφροσιν .
sections.translation: I warn you not to bind me , since I am in my senses and you are not .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: ἐγὼ δὲ δεῖν γε , κυριώτερος σέθεν .
sections.translation: And I , more masterful than you , bid them to bind you .

sections.label: Section 506
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθʼ ὅ τι ζῇς , οὐδʼ ὃ δρᾷς , οὐδʼ ὅστις εἶ .
sections.translation: You do not know why you live , or what you are doing , or who you are .

sections.label: Section 507
sections.original: Πενθεύς , Ἀγαύης παῖς , πατρὸς δʼ Ἐχίονος .
sections.translation: I am Pentheus , son of Echion and Agave .

sections.label: Section 508
sections.original: ἐνδυστυχῆσαι τοὔνομʼ ἐπιτήδειος εἶ .
sections.translation: You are well-suited to be miserable in your name . [*]

sections.label: Section 509
sections.original: χώρει · καθείρξατʼ αὐτὸν ἱππικαῖς πέλας
sections.translation: Go . To attendants Shut him up near the horse

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: φάτναισιν , ὡς ἂν σκότιον εἰσορᾷ κνέφας .
sections.translation: stable , so that he may see only darkness . To Dionysus Dance there ; and as for these women whom you have led here as accomplices to your crimes , we will either sell them or , stopping their hands from this noise and beating of skins , I will keep them as slaves at the loom .

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: στείχοιμʼ ἄν · ὅ τι γὰρ μὴ χρεών , οὔτοι χρεὼν
sections.translation: I will go , for I need not suffer that which is not necessary . But Dionysus , who you claim does not exist , will pursue you for these insults . For in injuring us , you put him in bonds .

sections.label: Section 1.519
sections.translation: ⋯ Daughter of Acheloüs ,

sections.label: Section 1.520
sections.original: πότνιʼ εὐπάρθενε Δίρκα ,
sections.translation: venerable Dirce , happy virgin , you once received the child of Zeus in your streams , when Zeus his father snatched him up from the immortal fire and saved him in his thigh ,

sections.label: Section 1.525
sections.original: πασέ νιν , τάδʼ ἀναβοάσας ·
sections.translation: crying out : Go , Dithyrambus , enter this my male womb . I will make you illustrious , Bacchus , in Thebes , so that they will call you by this name .

sections.label: Section 1.530
sections.original: σὺ δέ μʼ , ὦ μάκαιρα Δίρκα ,
sections.translation: But you , blessed Dirce , reject me with my garland-bearing company about you . Why do you refuse me , why do you flee me ? I swear by the cluster-bearing

sections.label: Section 1.535
sections.original: Διονύσου χάριν οἴνας ,
sections.translation: delight of Dionysus ’ vine that you will have a care for Bromius .

sections.label: Section 1.537
sections.original: οἵαν οἵαν ὀργὰν
sections.translation: What rage , what rage does the earth-born race show , and Pentheus ,

sections.label: Section 1.540
sections.original: ποτε Πενθεύς , ὃν Ἐχίων
sections.translation: once descended from a serpent — Pentheus , whom earth-born Echion bore , a fierce monster , not a mortal man , but like a bloody giant , hostile to the gods .

sections.label: Section 1.545
sections.original: ὃς ἔμʼ ἐν βρόχοισι τὰν τοῦ
sections.translation: He will soon bind me , the hand-maid of Bromius , in chains , and he already holds my fellow-reveler within the house , hidden in a dark prison .

sections.label: Section 1.550
sections.original: ἐσορᾷς τάδʼ , ὦ Διὸς παῖ
sections.translation: Do you see this , O Dionysus , son of Zeus , your priests in the dangers of restraint ? Come , lord , down from Olympus , brandishing your golden thyrsos ,

sections.label: Section 1.555
sections.original: φονίου δʼ ἀνδρὸς ὕβριν κατάσχες .
sections.translation: and restrain the insolence of the blood-thirsty man .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: πόθι Νύσας ἄρα τᾶς θη-
sections.translation: Where on Nysa , which nourishes wild beasts , or on Corycian heights , do you lead with your thyrsos the bands of revelers ?

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: τάχα δʼ ἐν ταῖς πολυδένδρεσ-
sections.translation: Perhaps in the deep-wooded lairs of Olympus , where Orpheus once playing the lyre drew together trees by his songs , drew together the beasts of the fields .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: μάκαρ ὦ Πιερία ,
sections.translation: Blessed Pieria , the Joyful one reveres you and will come to lead the dance in revelry ; having crossed the swiftly flowing Axius he will bring the

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: σομένας Μαινάδας ἄξει ,
sections.translation: whirling Maenads , leaving Lydias , giver of wealth to mortals , the father who they say fertilizes the land of beautiful horses with
